parentQuestion: Where would you NOT find a cholinergic nicotinic receptor?

scaffolds.q: There are TWO types of ACh receptors: Nicotinic and muscarinic.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Both respond to ACh, but they're structurally different and found in different locations.

scaffolds.q: Nicotinic receptors are ion channels that open directly when ACh binds.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Nicotinic receptors are ligand-gated ion channels - fast, excitatory responses.

scaffolds.q: Muscarinic receptors use G-protein signaling (slower, modulatory effects).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Muscarinic receptors are metabotropic (G-protein coupled) - they trigger intracellular cascades.

scaffolds.q: Nicotinic receptors are found at ALL autonomic ganglia (both divisions).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Preganglionic ACh activates nicotinic receptors on postganglionic neurons - universal in both sympathetic and parasympathetic ganglia.

scaffolds.q: Nicotinic receptors are found at neuromuscular junctions (skeletal muscle).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Motor neurons release ACh onto nicotinic receptors on skeletal muscle, causing contraction.

scaffolds.q: Nicotinic receptors are found on adrenal medulla chromaffin cells.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The adrenal medulla receives direct preganglionic sympathetic input via nicotinic receptors.

scaffolds.q: Parasympathetic TARGET ORGANS have MUSCARINIC receptors, not nicotinic.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Ganglia have nicotinic, but effector organs (heart, GI, glands) have muscarinic receptors for postganglionic ACh.

scaffolds.q: Nicotinic receptors are NOT found at parasympathetic target organs.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Target organs have muscarinic receptors. Nicotinic receptors are at ganglia, NMJs, and adrenal medulla - not at parasympathetic effector tissues.

scaffolds.q: The answer is 'parasympathetic target organs' - they have muscarinic, not nicotinic receptors.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! You would NOT find nicotinic receptors at parasympathetic target organs. Those tissues have muscarinic receptors for responding to postganglionic ACh.
